_comment: cherrypick orchestration config. Copy to config.json and adjust. A module is located by 'repo' (cloned by `cherrypick install` into ~/.cherrypick/modules/<repo-name>) or by an explicit 'path' override; paths are relative to this file's directory. Never hardcode absolute machine paths.
_home_note: Runtime home (this config.json, logs/, state/, dashboard.html) = CHERRYPICK_HOME if set, else the repo root in a source checkout, else ~/.cherrypick for a pip-installed copy. An installed copy thus reads ~/.cherrypick/config.json.
_modules_home_note: Managed module checkouts live under CHERRYPICK_MODULES_HOME, else CHERRYPICK_HOME/modules, else ~/.cherrypick/modules. `install` clones a module only when its checkout is absent and never touches an existing one; `uninstall` leaves checkouts in place.

meic._path_note: Locate the module by EITHER 'repo' (install clones it to the managed modules home) OR 'path' (an in-place dev checkout, absolute or relative to this file — never cloned over). Optional 'ref' picks a branch/tag to clone.
meic._keyring_note: The module's OS-keyring service name, used by `cherrypick account`/`connect` to read/select the module's live-trading account (ACCOUNT_NUMBER) via the shared CredentialStore. Optional 'keyring_legacy_services' lists read-only fallbacks (MEIC keeps 'tastytrade-mcp' for pre-rename creds). Omit to disable account selection for this module.

paper._paper_db_note: Path to the module's paper-trades SQLite DB — MUST point at the file the module actually WRITES (a mismatch silently blinds the orchestrator's report/reconcile/dashboard and the watchdog freshness check). Relative resolves against the module checkout; a leading '~' or env var expands (use e.g. '~/.cherrypick/data/meic/paper_trades.db' when the module keeps its data in the managed home); an absolute path is used as-is.

calibration._comment: Optional. `cherrypick calibrate` reads paper P&L per risk profile and advises promotion up this ladder (advisory only; never switches live risk). Omit 'ladder' to get readings without recommendations. 'deliberate_only' rungs are never auto-recommended; 'rule' overrides the default {min_days:14, min_win_rate:0.6, min_sample:20}.

paper._dolt_databases_note: Databases the runner needs served on dolt_host:dolt_port. doctor verifies each is present (not just that the port answers) when a MySQL client is available, catching a Dolt server rooted at the wrong data dir. Omit to check port reachability only.
paper._dolt_service_note: Optional cherrypick-managed keep-alive. `install` registers a MINUTE task that runs `ensure-dolt` (starts `dolt sql-server` from data_dir only when nothing is on the port); `uninstall` removes it; `status`/`doctor` surface it. data_dir is portable: `~` expands, a relative path resolves against the cherrypick runtime ROOT. Omit this block to manage Dolt yourself.

watchdog._drawdown_note: Optional paper-P&L drift alert (net-of-cost). Add a 'drawdown' block to enable: {"suite_floor": -1000, "module_floors": {"meic": -750, "earnings": -750}, "critical_multiplier": 2}. net<=floor -> WARN; net<=floor*critical_multiplier -> CRITICAL. Paper only; never trades. Omit to disable.

dashboard._comment: Read-only status dashboard: static self-contained HTML regenerated on each watchdog tick and by `cherrypick dashboard`. Surfaces suite health + P&L + logs, plus a System panel (scheduled tasks, installed modules, and a redacted config summary — webhook state only, never URLs). Under --serve it adds a live checks card that polls doctor.run() (the only broker-touching part, kept off the static path). 'output' is relative to this file's directory.

serve._comment: `cherrypick dashboard --serve` runs a localhost live view (same page rebuilt per request) instead of writing the static file. Loopback-only, read-only. Override host/port on the CLI with --host/--port.

sections._comment: Optional live cards for `dashboard --serve`. Each section is subprocessed for a cherrypick.core.viz payload and rendered generically (the orchestrator never imports the module). A new module gets a card by emitting the schema — add it here. Locate the module by 'path' (in-place checkout, relative to this file) or 'repo'. fetch_argv may use {symbol} etc. Deliberately NOT under 'modules' (that registry is for paper pipelines).
sections.title: GEX

embeds._comment: Optional embedded module dashboards for `dashboard --serve` only: each module's OWN full dashboard, shown in an iframe on the orchestrator page. The orchestrator drives the module by config-declared argv (never imports it) and PAPER mode must be forced in that argv. Locate the module by 'path' (relative to this file) or 'repo'. Two kinds: 'server' (module runs its own HTTP dashboard — orchestrator launches it if the port is down, iframe points at {port}) and 'static' (module regenerates a self-contained HTML file — orchestrator runs build_argv, throttled to refresh_seconds, and serves 'output'). Disabled by default.
embeds.title: MEIC

embeds._comment: The gex module's full GEX/IV-Skew/Volume dashboard (read-only off the shared stream cache). Complements the compact live GEX section card above.
embeds.title: GEX Dashboard

services._comment: Long-running background daemons the orchestrator keeps alive: started at `install`, restarted by the watchdog if down (single-instance guarded), stopped at `uninstall`. Located by 'path' (relative to this file) or 'repo'. status_argv must print {"running": bool}. The gex spot-trail recorder samples every symbol's spot continuously so the GEX dashboard's spot trail has no gaps and builds all session, independent of whether the dashboard is open. Disabled by default.

trade_notify._comment: Dedicated low-latency task that pushes new paper entries/exits to trade_channels. Runs far more often than the watchdog so fills reach you within ~interval_minutes instead of a full watchdog cycle.

eod_digest._comment: Suite end-of-day digest. Once per afternoon a scheduled task writes logs/eod-digest-<day>.md (one session's cross-module paper P&L, net of costs, plus a pointer to each module's own paper-eod file) and pushes a one-line summary through your notify channels. ON by default and registered by `install`. To OPT OUT set enabled=false, then re-run `install` (it will skip the task) or `uninstall` (it removes any previously-registered one). 'at' is the box's local clock (assumed ET, like the modules' entry_time/exit_time); 16:15 lands just after MEIC's 16:00 settlement so both modules' files exist. Read-only over the paper DBs; off the watchdog reliability path.

reconcile._comment: `cherrypick reconcile` (and the serve-only /api/reconcile dashboard card): a paper<->live isolation guard. It enumerates EVERY account on the login (list_accounts - tastytrade returns multiple per user) and checks each (read-only get_positions/get_account_info), flagging any open positions/BP a paper-only suite shouldn't have. On-demand and off the watchdog reliability path; never trades; account numbers masked. Optional 'broker_module' forces which module's tt.py to query; omit to auto-discover the first positions-capable one.

notify._channels_note: push channels: 'log' (always on, the floor), 'desktop', 'slack', 'discord'. Slack/Discord webhook URLs live in the OS keyring (cherrypick secrets-set --channel slack|discord), never in this file or env vars.
